subject: FW: New Year's greetings
body:
From: RF3519@aol.com [mailto:RF3519@aol.com] 
Sent: Wednesday, January 02, 2008 3:25 PM
To: bfradd@yahoo.com; jack@gillfndt.org; Lesley Groff;
cbienstock@carlinamerica.com; rbienstock@carlinamerica.com;
jamie@jlsingleton.com; robbie@nightlymoon.com;
young@helix.mgh.harvard.edu; nzervas@partners.org;
caviness@helix.mgh.harvard.edu; chirp@wildsandcutuary.com;
sally@airstudios.com; rectory@dircon.co.uk; dhubel@hms.harvard.edu;
M.Gazzaniga@psych.ucsb.edu; ldbraida@mit.edu
Cc: mtramo@hms.harvard.edu
Subject: New Year's greetings

 

Dear Friends,

 

Though I had intended to send best wishes for the New Year during the
last week of December, I was laid low by a virus of some sort during the
final week of the old year, though I am feeling a lot better this
afternoon, at last.

 

I write to thank you all for your help and support in what seems to me
the very important work of Mark Tramo and his colleagues at the
Institute for Music and Brian Science at Harvard-MGH.  A professional
musician and music teacher for the past half century (and the son and
grandson of professional musicians), I have for many years thought  that
a better understanding of how the human brain facilitates and reacts to
music would be of huge benefit both to humanity and to the future of
music's influence on human kind.  Thus, I remain very enthusiastic about
the long term prospects of the Institute's work, and am deeply grateful
to you all for your continuing friendship and support of that good work.

 

Please never hesitate to call upon me if there are prospects which you
believe Mark and I should attend to, in the near or middle term.  Both
of us are ever eager to hear from you, by telephone or email, as suites
your convenience.

 

Warm best wishes for a healthy and productive 2008,

 

Sincerely,

 

Bob

 

 

Robert Freeman

Susan Menefee Ragan Regents Professor of Fine Arts

Director, Bryce Jordan Arts Entrepreneurship Incubator

co-director: The Utunes Project (see www.webapp.utexas.edu/wp/utunes)

The University of Texas at Austin

512-471-8409; rsfreeman@mail.utexas.edu
<mailto:rsfreeman@mail.utexas.edu> : office

512-338-4143, rf3519@aol.com: home





________________________________

for winter.
body_html:
From: RF3519@aol.com [mailto:RF3519@aol.com]
Sent: Wednesday, January 02, 2008 3:25 PM
To: bfradd@yahoo.com; jack@gillfndt.org; Lesley Groff; cbienstock@carlinamerica.com; rbienstock@carlinamerica.com; jamie@jlsingleton.com; robbie@nightlymoon.com; young@helix.mgh.harvard.edu; nzervas@partners.org; caviness@helix.mgh.harvard.edu; chirp@wildsandcutuary.com; sally@airstudios.com; rectory@dircon.co.uk; dhubel@hms.harvard.edu; M.Gazzaniga@psych.ucsb.edu; ldbraida@mit.edu
Cc: mtramo@hms.harvard.edu
Subject: New Year's greetings

 

Dear Friends,

 

Though I had intended to send best wishes for the New Year during the last week of December, I was laid low by a virus of some sort during the final week of the old year, though I am feeling a lot better this afternoon, at last.

 

I write to thank you all for your help and support in what seems to me the very important work of Mark Tramo and his colleagues at the Institute for Music and Brian Science at Harvard-MGH.  A professional musician and music teacher for the past half century (and the son and grandson of professional musicians), I have for many years thought  that a better understanding of how the human brain facilitates and reacts to music would be of huge benefit both to humanity and to the future of music's influence on human kind.  Thus, I remain very enthusiastic about the long term prospects of the Institute's work, and am deeply grateful to you all for your continuing friendship and support of that good work.

 

Please never hesitate to call upon me if there are prospects which you believe Mark and I should attend to, in the near or middle term.  Both of us are ever eager to hear from you, by telephone or email, as suites your convenience.

 

Warm best wishes for a healthy and productive 2008,

 

Sincerely,

 

Bob

 

 

Robert Freeman

Susan Menefee Ragan Regents Professor of Fine Arts

Director, Bryce Jordan Arts Entrepreneurship Incubator

co-director: The Utunes Project (see www.webapp.utexas.edu/wp/utunes)

The University of Texas at Austin
